meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn how to implement advanced analytics to validate demand and solve 'no project ideas' using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, instrument your landing page with event-based analytics to capture user intent. By tracking feature clicks and friction points, you transform 'no ideas' into data-driven pivots. Use Antigravity to scaffold your tracking stack and Claude Code to automate the deployment of your event schema.
content.intro:
The empty canvas is the founder’s greatest adversary. You sit staring at a blank repository, waiting for a 'Eureka' moment that never comes because you are building in a vacuum. The secret isn't more brainstorming; it's radical transparency into user behavior. 

By deploying an analytics-first architecture, you turn your MVP into a laboratory. You stop guessing what people want and start measuring what they actually click. This tutorial bridges the gap between 'no project ideas' and a validated SaaS product by leveraging automated tooling to track the only metric that matters: intent.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three events that prove product-market fit. This ensures you aren't just tracking noise, but actionable signals.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current landing page structure and suggest a schema for tracking 'Conversion Intent' events, specifically focusing on button clicks and scroll depth that indicate high-intent interest.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a JSON schema for PostHog events based on my landing page components.'

steps.title: Automated Event Instrumentation
steps.description: Manually coding event handlers is a waste of founder time. Use AI to inject tracking hooks into your components automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my React components to include analytics tracking hooks using the PostHog standard. Ensure every CTA has a unique data-attribute for tracking.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --files src/components/ --instruction 'Inject analytics tracking to all buttons and form submissions.'

steps.title: Deploying the Analytics Layer
steps.description: Integrate your analytics provider as a global service. This keeps your business logic clean while ensuring data flows seamlessly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a provider wrapper for my application that initializes the analytics client and captures automatic page views without interfering with page load speeds.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create an analytics provider component and wrap it around the main application root.'

steps.title: Validate with Heatmaps
steps.description: Visual data often reveals friction points your code can't see. Automate the setup of session recording triggers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure session recording triggers to only activate when a user spends more than 30 seconds on the pricing page, to optimize for cost and clarity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Add session recording configuration to the analytics initialization file.'

steps.title: Automated Feedback Loops
steps.description: Set up alerts that notify you when high-intent events occur, turning your analytics into a lead generation machine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that monitors my analytics API and sends a Slack notification whenever a user completes a 'Sign-up Intent' event.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a serverless function that triggers a webhook alert on specific analytics events.'

pro_tips: Track 'Negative Signals'—if a user clicks a feature button and immediately leaves, that's a signal to pivot, not a bug to fix.
Use anonymous ID tracking before login to bridge the gap between anonymous visitors and converted customers.
Don't bloat your bundle; use a proxy for your analytics script to improve core web vitals and bypass ad-blockers.
Implement 'Feature Flags' linked to analytics; toggle UI elements based on real-time usage data without deploying new code.
Focus on 'Time-to-Value' metrics; measure how long it takes a user to reach their first 'Aha!' moment and optimize everything else for that path.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, run traffic to your landing page for 48 hours. Analyze the 'drop-off' points and use those insights to refine your MVP features. If the data shows low intent, kill the idea immediately and move to the next one.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start engineering for revenue. With these tools, you'll never wonder what to build again because your users will tell you exactly what they're willing to pay for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
